facts.text: Endicott was founded as a company town by the Endicott-Johnson Corporation, becoming the largest shoe company worldwide by WWI.
The village was named in honor of Henry B. Endicott, a Massachusetts shoemaker who bought the company in 1890.
In 1911, the Computing-Tabulating-Recording Company, later IBM, was founded in Endicott, launching a century of tech innovation.
The Endicott History & Heritage Center, run by the Old Village of Union Historical Society, showcases extensive displays on both EJ and IBM.
During World War II, several IBM factories in Endicott were converted to produce sidearm pistols for the war effort.
The village’s historic business district sits at the intersection of NYS Route 26 and NYS Route 17C, once the original Union village center.
